title: "Intel DG43NB motherboard and sensors config"
date: 2009-11-10
forum: Hardware
---

### Post by clivelr on 2009-11-10
Hi all,

OS: Karmic 64 Bit

I am trying to setup lm-sensors on my pc. It is working (of sorts) and I am getting readings, however I am unable to get the "_min" and "_max" settings in /etc/sensors3.conf to work.

my /etc/default/sensors looks like this (Removed comment lines):
```
SYSLOG_FACILITY=daemon
# Libsensors config file to use
CONFIG_FILE=/etc/sensors3.conf
```

Relevant portion of /etc/modules:
```
# Generated by sensors-detect on Wed Nov 11 09:49:49 2009
# Chip drivers
w83627ehf
coretemp
```

Output from sensors:
```
w83627dhg-isa-0290
Adapter: ISA adapter
VCore:       +2.04 V  (min =  +2.04 V, max =  +2.04 V)   ALARM
[COLOR="Red"]in1:        +13.46 V  (min = +13.46 V, max = +13.46 V)   ALARM[/COLOR]
AVCC:        +4.08 V  (min =  +4.08 V, max =  +4.08 V)   ALARM
3VCC:        +4.08 V  (min =  +4.08 V, max =  +4.08 V)   ALARM
in4:         +2.04 V  (min =  +2.04 V, max =  +2.04 V)   ALARM
in5:         +2.04 V  (min =  +2.04 V, max =  +2.04 V)   ALARM
in6:         +6.53 V  (min =  +6.53 V, max =  +6.53 V)   ALARM
VSB:         +4.08 V  (min =  +4.08 V, max =  +4.08 V)   ALARM
VBAT:        +4.08 V  (min =  +4.08 V, max =  +4.08 V)   ALARM
Sys Temp:     -1.0Â°C  (high =  -1.0Â°C, hyst =  -1.0Â°C)  ALARM  sensor = diode
CPU Temp:     +0.0Â°C  (high =  +0.0Â°C, hyst =  +0.0Â°C)  ALARM  sensor = diode
AUX Temp:     +0.0Â°C  (high =  +0.0Â°C, hyst =  +0.0Â°C)  ALARM  sensor = diode
cpu0_vid:   +2.050 V

coretemp-isa-0000
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 0:      +41.0Â°C  (high = +78.0Â°C, crit = +100.0Â°C)

coretemp-isa-0001
Adapter: ISA adapter
Core 1:      +41.0Â°C  (high = +78.0Â°C, crit = +100.0Â°C)
```

As you can see for example the in1 has a min of 13.46 and a max of 13.46, and will be alarming every time it is checked.

I have tried setting the min/max in /etc/sensors3.conf:
```
chip "w83627ehf-*" "w83627dhg-*"

    label in0 "VCore"
    label in2 "AVCC"
    label in3 "3VCC"
    label in7 "VSB"
    label in8 "VBAT"

# +12V is in1 and +5V is in6 as recommended by datasheet
    compute in1 @*(1+(56/10)),  @/(1+(56/10))
    compute in6 @*(1+(22/10)),  @/(1+(22/10))
[COLOR="Red"]    set in1_min   10
    set in1_max   15[/COLOR]
```
Sensors still reports a min and max of 13.46.
```
[COLOR="Blue"][COLOR="DarkOrchid"]root@thismachine:~#[/COLOR] sensors -s; sensors
w83627dhg-isa-0290
Adapter: ISA adapter
VCore:       +2.04 V  (min =  +2.04 V, max =  +2.04 V)   ALARM
[COLOR="Red"]in1:        +13.46 V  (min = +13.46 V, max = +13.46 V)   ALARM[/COLOR]
.
.
.[/COLOR]
```


Any ideas/help would be really appreciated.

P.S. I have another pc, with a P5K Deluxe motherboard, but which uses the same chipset within the /etc/sensors3.conf. On this one I have just changed the min/max values and it seems to work fine.
```
w83627dhg-isa-0290
Adapter: ISA adapter
VCore:       +1.13 V  (min =  +0.00 V, max =  +1.74 V)
in1:        +11.35 V  (min =  +7.50 V, max = +12.09 V)
AVCC:        +3.30 V  (min =  +3.14 V, max =  +3.47 V)
3VCC:        +3.30 V  (min =  +3.14 V, max =  +3.47 V)
in4:         +1.56 V  (min =  +1.10 V, max =  +2.04 V)
in5:         +1.70 V  (min =  +1.02 V, max =  +2.04 V)
in6:         +5.09 V  (min =  +4.76 V, max =  +5.25 V)
VSB:         +3.30 V  (min =  +3.14 V, max =  +3.47 V)
VBAT:        +3.28 V  (min =  +3.14 V, max =  +3.47 V)

```
